DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-02-2021 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ANDREW C. MCCLELLAND, Esq. of Lunski & Stimpert LLP (Tel: 415-426-3525) for plaintiff;
Atty. NORA R. BOARDMAN, Esq. of Law Offices of John A. Hauser (Tel: 714-571-0407) for defendants Sequoia Property Services, Inc. and Thaddeus Dayton.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS THADDEUS DAYTON AND SEQUOIA PROPERTY SERVICES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant SEQUOIA PROPERTY SERVICES, INC.'s demurrers to cause of action 4 for intentional misrepresentation and cause of action 5 for false promise are overruled.

Paragraphs 41 and 49 of the First Amended Complaint plead the fraud allegations with sufficient particularity. Plaintiff alleges that defendant made oral false representations to it at a meeting on November 6, 2013. Plaintiff specifically identifies the multiple false statements. While plaintiff originally believed that Mr. Dayton may have had a part in making those representations and now believes he did not, defendant Sequoia should be aware of its representative that it sent to the meeting. Less specificity is required where "the facts lie more in the knowledge of the opposite party." (Tenet HealthSystem Desert, Inc. v. Blue Cross of Cal. (2016) 245 Cal.App.4th 821, 838.) Plaintiff has "provide[d] enough information for respondents to know what purported falsehoods they must defend against." (Murphy v. BDO Seidman, LLP (2003) 113 Cal.App.4th 687, 693.) The Court further notes that defendant's attempt to attack these claims through declarations is improper on demurrer. "Because a demurrer challenges defects on the face of the complaint, it can refer to matters outside the pleading only if those matters are subject to judicial notice." (Tenet, 245 Cal.App.4th at 834 (emphasis in original).)

Defendant THADDEUS DAYTON's demurrers are off calendar as moot as he has been dismissed from this action.

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff First Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Dec-02-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ANDREW C. MCCLELLAND, Esq. of Lunski & Stimpert LLP (Tel: 415-426-3525) for plaintiff;
Atty. NORA R. BOARDMAN, Esq. of Law Offices of John A. Hauser (Tel: 714-571-0407) for defendants Sequoia Property Services, Inc. and Thaddeus Dayton.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS THADDEUS DAYTON AND SEQUOIA PROPERTY SERVICES, INC.'s Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff First Amended Complaint.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant SEQUOIA PROPERTY SERVICES, INC.'s motion to strike is denied. The amended complaint sufficiently pleads the fraud claims, which support the punitive damages allegations. The motion is moot as to THADDEUS DAYTON.

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
